zh.Hospital, County Limerick, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 利默里克霍斯皮塔爾
zh.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare於大約1550年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 克里。她於大約1600年與Nicholas Howard在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1605年10月在英國享年56歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.Hospital, County Limerick, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 利默里克霍斯皮塔爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare于大约1550年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 克里。她于大约1600年与Nicholas Howard在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1605年10月在英国享年56岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: O'Sullivan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Declan, Kieran, Cathal, Dermot, Eamon, Fergus, Grainne, Seamus.
Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Súileabháin ‘descendant of Súileabhán’, a personal name from súil ‘eye’ + dubh ‘dark’ + the diminutive suffix -án. Compare Sullivan .

variants.name: Sullivan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Donal, James Patrick, Michael Patrick, Aileen, Brigid, Paddy, Siobhan, John Patrick, Kevin Patrick, Parnell.
Irish (Cork and Kerry): shortened form of   O'Sullivan  .

nameMeanings.name: Beare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Beer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erhard, Hans, Heinz, Aloys, Bodo, Erna, Ernst, Frieda, Friedhelm, Hanni.

English (West Country): habitational name from any of the forty or so places in southwestern England called Beer(e) or Bear(e). Most of these derive their names from the West Saxon dative case, beara, of Old English bearu ‘grove, wood’ (the standard Old English dative bearwe being preserved in Barrow ). In some cases the surname may be topographic in origin, from atte beare ‘at the grove’. Some may be from Middle English bere ‘woodland swine-pasture’ (Old English bǣr). Compare Bear 1.

nameMeanings.name: Sheila
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Síle, the Irish Gaelic form of Cecily . This name has become so common and widespread that it is hardly felt to be Irish any longer. In Australia since the 19th century it has been a slang generic term for any woman.

variants.name: Sheela
meanings: ‘good character; piety’; in Buddhism one of the six perfections to be striven for.

variants.name: Sheelah
meanings: Pronounced ‘shee-la’; derived in the early Middle Ages from Anglo-Norman Cecile. See Cecily .

variants.name: Shelagh
meanings: Variant of   Sheila .  The final consonants in the written form seem to have been added to give a Gaelic feel to the name. There is no etymological justification for them.

variants.name: Síle
meanings: Pronounced ‘shee-la’; derived in the early Middle Ages from Anglo-Norman Cecile. See Cecily .

variants.name: Sheelagh
meanings: Pronounced ‘shee-la’; derived in the early Middle Ages from Anglo-Norman Cecile. See Cecily .

nameMeanings.name: Julia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the old Roman family name   Julius .  A woman called Julia is mentioned in Paul's Epistle to the Romans (Romans 16:15), and the name was borne by numerous early saints. Its frequency increased with the vogue for classical names in the 18th century, and it continues to enjoy considerable popularity, although the recent introduction of   Julie   to the English-speaking world has reduced this somewhat. Well-known bearers include the British actress  Julia Foster  (  b. 1941 ) and American actress  Julia Roberts  (  b. 1967  as  Julie Fiona Roberts ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Accession of James I. Surrender of Hugh O'Neill. Enforcement of English Law in Ireland.

en.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare was born about 1550, in County Kerry, Ireland. She married Nicholas Howard about 1600, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in October 1605, in England, at the age of 56, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare est née environ 1550, à County Kerry, Irlande. Elle a épousé Nicholas Howard environ 1600, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en octobre 1605, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare wurde um 1550 in County Kerry, Irland geboren. Sie hat um 1600 in England, United Kingdom Nicholas Howard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb im Oktober 1605 im Alter von 56 Jahren in England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare è nata il circa 1550 nel County Kerry, Irlanda. Lei sposato Nicholas Howard circa 1600, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel ottobre 1605, nel England, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

アイルランドコウントイ ケリー
ja.Of Molahuff, Kerry,, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ ケリー
ja.Hospital, County Limerick, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ リメリックホースパタル
ja.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beareは約1550年にアイルランドコウントイ ケリーで生まれた。約1600年にイギリスイギリスでNicholas Howardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1605年10月にイギリスで56歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare(은)는 대략 1550년 아일랜드 카운티 케리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1600년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Nicholas Howard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1605년 10월 영국에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare nasceu aproximadamente 1550, em County Kerry, Irlanda. Ela casou-se com Nicholas Howard aproximadamente 1600, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em outubro de 1605, em Inglaterra, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare родилась около 1550 г. в местности коунти кирри, Ирландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas Howard около 1600 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла октябрь 1605 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Sheila Julia O'Sullivan Beare nació aproximadamente 1550, en County Kerry, Irlanda. Se casó con Nicholas Howard aproximadamente 1600, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en octubre de 1605, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
